scenarios.description: Empty stylesheet triggers zero-iteration loop (hasNext false)
scenarios.expectedResult: CssContentHandler.startDocument() called once; CssContentHandler.endDocument() called once; no errors
pseudocode: CssErrorHandler err = stub; CssContentHandler doc = spy;
verify doc.startDocument() once; verify doc.endDocument() once; verifyNoInteractions(err);

scenarios.description: Single AT-rule with no block ends loop after first iteration
scenarios.precondition: Use StringReader with "@charset \"UTF-8\";"; real scan, stub handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument(), doc.startAtRule("charset"), doc.endAtRule("charset"), doc.endDocument(); no exceptions
pseudocode: CssErrorHandler err = stub; CssContentHandler doc = spy;

scenarios.description: Single ruleset triggers handleRuleSet path when token type != ATKEYWORD
scenarios.precondition: Use Reader with "h1 { color:red; }"; real scan, stub handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument(), doc.selectors(list with one selector), doc.declaration(...), doc.endSelectors(...), doc.endDocument(); no errors
pseudocode: CssErrorHandler err = stub; CssContentHandler doc = spy;
verify doc.selectors(...) called once; verify doc.declaration(...) called at least once;

scenarios.description: Two top-level constructs exercises loop of count 2 with ATKEYWORD then RULESET
scenarios.precondition: Reader with "@import url(x.css); h2 {margin:0;}"; stub handlers
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startAtRule("import"); doc.endAtRule("import"); then doc.selectors(...); doc.endSelectors(...); endDocument
pseudocode: TEST TC04 parse mixed at-rule and ruleset
Reader reader = new StringReader("@import url(x.css); h2 {margin:0;}" );

scenarios.description: PrematureEOFException in scan propagates IOException path
scenarios.precondition: Reader stub throws IOException on read(); mocking necessary to simulate IO error
scenarios.expectedResult: IOException is thrown; no calls to doc.startDocument()
pseudocode: throws IOException; verify doc.startDocument() never called;

scenarios.description: CssException in scan propagates CssException path
scenarios.precondition: Mock CssScanner.scan to throw CssException via stub token consumer; mocking necessary to simulate grammar error early
scenarios.expectedResult: CssException is thrown; no document events
pseudocode: Reader reader= new StringReader("bad"); mock CssParser.scan to throw CssException;

scenarios.description: PrematureEOF during handleRuleSet breaks loop and ends document
scenarios.precondition: Reader with "h1 { color" (missing ';' and '}') to trigger NoSuchElementException in handleRuleSet; real scan
scenarios.expectedResult: parse returns normally after break; doc.startDocument(), doc.endDocument(); err.error called once with GRAMMAR_PREMATURE_EOF
pseudocode: TEST TC07 parse ruleset premature EOF
verify err.error(...) called with GRAMMAR_PREMATURE_EOF; verify doc.endDocument() called;

scenarios.description: Handle selector-list error path when selectors==null in handleRuleSet
scenarios.precondition: Inject stub CssSelectorConstructFactory.createSelectorList(...) returns null; mocking necessary to simulate factory failure
scenarios.expectedResult: iter.next(MATCH_CLOSEBRACE) invoked; no doc.selectors or declarations; doc.endDocument() called
pseudocode: Reader reader=new StringReader("x{};"); mock factory.createSelectorList to return null;
verify doc.selectors not called; verify doc.endDocument() called;

scenarios.description: Use CssSource overload delegates to Reader overload
scenarios.precondition: Implement CssSource stub with newReader() returning empty reader and getSystemID="sys"; real parse(reader)
scenarios.expectedResult: Same as empty Reader: startDocument and endDocument only
pseudocode: TEST TC09 parse CssSource overload empty
CssSource src=stub with newReader()->empty reader, getSystemID()->"sys";

scenarios.description: Mixed two rulesets exercises multiple branch-false iterations
scenarios.expectedResult: Two selectors lists and two endSelectors calls; endDocument after both
pseudocode: verify doc.selectors called twice; verify doc.endSelectors called twice;
